feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch
entry.summary:
*Updated at 10:55pm to lift the Watch for eastern Bay of Plenty (including Rotorua) and northern Taupo, and to extend the validity for the remaining areas to 1am Saturday*.
A showery unstable air mass continues to affect parts of the North Island tonight. For inland Bay of Plenty west of Rotorua and southeastern Waikato there is a moderate risk of localised downpours from heavy showers and/or thunderstorms (rainfall intensities of 25 to 40 mm/h).
Rainfall of this intensity can cause surface and/or flash flooding, especially about low-lying areas such as streams, rivers or narrow valleys, and may also lead to slips. Driving conditions will also be hazardous with surface flooding and poor visibility in heavy rain.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
